title: pulicaria paludosa subsp. inuloides
intro: Pulicaria paludosa subsp. inuloides, also known as Marsh Fleabane, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is a perennial herb characterized by its yellow flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Pulicaria paludosa subsp. inuloides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of the world.
